authoritative_sources.title: 19 U.S.C. 1313, Drawback and Refunds
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the Law Revision Counsel, United States Code
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. statute defining drawback eligibility categories, refunds, substitutions, exports, destructions, and claim prerequisites.

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 190, Modernized Drawback
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary drawback regulation for notices, waivers, claims, complete claim requirements, accelerated payment, liquidation, and procedures.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Claim preparation, filing, and post-filing control

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 163, Recordkeeping
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary recordkeeping and auditability regulation for retaining, producing, and examining customs records that support drawback claims.

authoritative_sources.title: Drawback
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CBP drawback program guidance explaining practical program structure, claim categories, and operator-facing workflow concepts.

authoritative_sources.title: Drawback in ACE Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CBP FAQ that clarifies how drawback processing behaves in ACE, including transitions, filing assumptions, and operator constraints.

authoritative_sources.title: ACE Drawback CATAIR
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CBP system specification for ACE drawback transmission layouts, status messages, edits, and error handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Electronic filing, response handling, and rework

authoritative_sources.title: FinCEN Advisory on Trade-Based Money Laundering
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury, Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Treasury AML guidance describing trade-based money laundering indicators that can affect drawback refund and export-linkage risk handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Fraud, AML, and suspicious refund escalation

authoritative_sources.title: WCO Data Model
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized customs data standard used to normalize cross-system trade data elements and constrain evidence linkage across filing records.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and prerequisite capture
workflow_stages.description: Confirm claimant identity, drawback provision, import and export or destruction context, tenant authority, and jurisdictional facts before any claim progression can be trusted.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Notice, waiver, and support readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether prior notice, waiver, complete claim support, substitution logic, and supporting documents are sufficient for the intended drawback path.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Claim filing and system response handling
workflow_stages.description: Prepare drawback filing packages, transmit through ACE, interpret edits or errors, and route rework when transmission or support states block progression.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Accelerated payment, liquidation, and post-filing follow-up
workflow_stages.description: Track when accelerated payment controls, bond support, liquidation status, or post-filing discrepancies change the allowed next action or prevent closure.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and escalation routing
workflow_stages.description: Produce drawback execution records, completion evidence, and authority-safe escalation packages without claiming legal, fraud, or payment signoff.

artifacts: Drawback claim packets, claim numbers, and line-level schedules
Import entry summaries, CBP Form 7501 records, and duty, tax, and fee payment evidence
Proof of exportation, destruction records, bills of lading, AES evidence, and inventory movement support
Manufacturing or substitution records, MTIN linkages, bills of materials, and allocation schedules
CBP Form 7553 notices of intent, waiver approvals, and supporting-document references
ACE drawback transmissions, error responses, DIS attachments, and broker workpapers
Accelerated payment enrollment, bond records, liquidation history, and refund tracking notes
Communication logs, prior disclosure notes, finance tie-outs, and approval history
systems_of_record: Automated Broker Interface (ABI) drawback filing connections
Broker drawback platform or customs operating system
ERP, inventory, manufacturing, and export documentation systems
General ledger, treasury, and reconciliation systems
Case-management, document management, and workpaper repositories
Fraud, AML, and compliance escalation systems

allowed: Determine whether available records support drawback workflow progression, hold, rework, or completion.
Produce execution summaries, evidence checklists, remediation notes, and escalation packets tied to named drawback records.
Identify when notice, waiver, complete claim support, accelerated payment, or liquidation follow-up is the correct next step.
escalate: A statutory interpretation, ruling, prior disclosure issue, or tenant legal policy materially changes the answer.
Suspicious export linkage, unusual counterparties, or refund-value anomalies indicate fraud, AML, or sanctions risk.
Ledger, refund, entry, and claim records conflict and require finance or compliance reconciliation.
refuse: The request asks for final legal signoff, drawback eligibility approval, protest strategy, or refund authorization outside the lane.
Required import, export, destruction, substitution, or tenant authority records are missing and would change the result.
The request requires a fabricated proof of export, destruction support, claim linkage, or unsupported override of documented policy.

domain_failure_modes: Treating import and export records as linked without traceable line-level evidence or an auditable substitution basis.
Claiming complete-claim status when notice, waiver, supporting documents, or manufacturing records are missing.
Closing a drawback case despite contradictions between ACE statuses, broker workpapers, and finance refund tie-outs.
Confusing operational filing support with legal interpretation, prior disclosure advice, or final drawback eligibility authority.
Allowing accelerated-payment or refund-completion steps to proceed without bond, control, or tenant approval evidence.
Missing TBML or sanctions signals embedded in export routes, counterparties, or refund anomalies.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution with realistic terminology
Low-confidence handling when prerequisites are missing

unresolved_ambiguities.explanation: Public sources establish drawback processing and claim evidence, but the tenant must supply who can approve refund release, reimbursement offsets, or finance disbursement changes.
